steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 makes a broad claim: Marisol Vega draws on "a variety of Mexican musical genres." Sentence 2 names specific genres — corridos, rancheras, and other northern genres. The blank must signal that sentence 2 narrows down to concrete examples of the general claim in sentence 1 (specification).

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Specifically,’: signals that what follows is a concrete illustration of the preceding general claim, which is exactly what sentence 2 does by naming the particular genres. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Therefore,’: signals a logical consequence or result. Sentence 2 is not a consequence of sentence 1; it is an elaboration. Cause-effect rule violated. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In conclusion,’: signals a final summary wrapping up prior points. Sentence 2 opens an elaboration, not a conclusion. Logical sequence rule violated. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Rather,’: signals contrast or correction. Sentence 2 does not contradict sentence 1; it illustrates it. Contrast rule violated. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
